stem: In triangle GHI, point J lies on side GH and point K lies on side GI so that triangle GJK is similar to triangle GHI, with G, J, and K corresponding to G, H, and I, respectively. If the measure of angle GJK is 64°, what is the measure of angle GHI?
rationale: Choice D is correct. In similar triangles, corresponding angles are congruent. Since angle GJK corresponds to angle GHI (both are at the same relative vertex in the similarity correspondence), m∠GHI = m∠GJK = 64°. Choices A and B are incorrect arithmetic combinations. Choice C is the complement, not the angle itself.
